en_body:
I pour myself a glass of cold water
I spread my books and papers on the table
a fistful of pencils, pens and inks of every color
I turn on the little lamp,
I sit down to study...
I open a feast to celebrate my birthday
 with
my friends and my enemies:
paper, pens, ink, and me.
I do not understand why the days upon the calendar
The numbers on the wall, inside the books
Still cannot add up to my age
And the numbers remain always vague and unimportant
Yet why in pain I see myself
Shrinking, body numb, still writhing with one private sorrow!...
(My 21st birthday, Houston, Texas)

Birthday Song
I pour myself a glass of cold water
I spread my books and papers on the table
a fistful of pencils, pens and inks of every color
I turn on the little lamp,
I sit down to study...
I open a feast to celebrate my birthday
 with
my friends and my enemies:
paper, pens, ink, and me.
I do not understand why the days upon the calendar
The numbers on the wall, inside the books
Still cannot add up to my age
And the numbers remain always vague and unimportant
Yet why in pain I see myself
Shrinking, body numb, still writhing with one private sorrow!...
(My 21st birthday, Houston, Texas)
